rulings.Case Title: ERIC DEBBANE ET AL VS. CITY & COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 31, 2024 line 1. DEFENDANT CITY & COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, BEN ROSENFELD, JOSE CISNEROS MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication is moot. Plaintiff's motion for summary judgment granted. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: GWEN TON ET AL VS. WESTCO ROOFING COMPANY, INC., A CALIFORNIA ET AL

DEFENDANT WESTCO ROOFING COMPANY, INC., BERKELEY ROOF SERVICES, INC., TAE LEE MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is GRANTED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts, not conclusions, in support of malice, oppression or fraud. rulings.Case Title: GWEN TON ET AL VS. WESTCO ROOFING COMPANY, INC., A CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 31, 2024 line 3. DEFENDANT WESTCO ROOFING COMPANY, INC., BERKELEY ROOF SERVICES, INC., TAE LEE DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. Demurrer to the entire complaint by Lee and Berkeley Roof Services, Inc is overruled. Allegations in the complaint must be taken as true at the pleading stage. Demurrer to the cause of action for premises liability is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts supporting this cause of action as to each defendant showing how each defendant was in possession and control of the premises/or part thereof on April 18, 2022. Demurrer to the cause of action for IIED asserted by corporate Plaintiffs is sustained without leave to amend. No opposition filed. Demurrer to the cause of action for IIED by other Plaintiffs is sustained with leave to amend for each Plaintiff to allege facts in support of each element of this cause of action to each Defendant. Plaintiff shall not make any generalized allegations as to all "Roofing Defendants" and shall make specific allegations as to each defendant. To the extent Plaintiff relied on alter ego liability Plaintiff must allege all elements of alter ego with requisite specificity. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: LAWRENCE T. LUI ET AL VS. GARY LUI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment For Possession Of Real Property
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 31, 2024 line 4. PLAINTIFF LAWRENCE LUI , AS CO-TRUSTEES OF THE LUI FAMILY TRUST DATED AUGUST 27, 2005, TERRY LUI, GLORIA LEE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment For Possession Of Real Property is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion for Summary Judgment is DENIED. Plaintiff moves for summary judgment on the same grounds as in the previously denied motions. (Compare Notices of Motion filed on September 20, 2024 and October 9, 2024: 1:21-28). Plaintiff points to no "newly discovered [after September 20, 2024] facts or circumstances" or "change of law" that occurred on or after September 20, 2024. CCP 437c(f)(2). Motion for Summary Adjudication of Affirmative Defenses is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion is GRANTED as to the affirmative defenses for breach of warranty of habitability and Violation of Fair Debt Collection Practices Act. Motion is denied as to the affirmative defenses of retaliation, estoppel, unclean hands and lack of standing. While Plaintiff claims that defendants have no evidence to support these defenses, Plaintiff fails to point to any allegedly factually devoid discovery responses in either the Points and Authorities or in the Separate Statement. Motion for Summary Adjudication is DENIED as to the SFRO affirmative defenses. Triable issues of fact exist as to Defendants' statutes (licensees vs. tenants). See Lui Deposition transcript pages 21 and 22. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
